title: Sprint stuck in review-loop: reviewer-morty re-reviews same in_review tickets each iteration instead of marking blocked after N failed gates
source_context: sprint --auto --smart-routing on RickIDE project, 19 todos, 3 done before loop started, then 0 progress for iters 11-13
description: Observed in RickIDE sprint 2026-06-05 (full sprint, 50 iter cap). Iterations #11-13 all show identical sequence: reviewer-morty inspects RICK-017/018/019, fails 'quality gate: missing files_changed or description', leaves status as 'in_review'. Next iteration: same three tickets, same failure, same outcome. Sprint burns iters with zero progress. Should: detect N>2 consecutive review failures on same ticket and auto-move to BLOCKED with reviewer's reason, freeing the sprint to pick up other todos.
proposed_changes: Track per-ticket review_attempt_count in orchestrate.py sprint loop. After 2-3 consecutive failures with same gate-reason, call ticket.py blocked --reason=last_gate_msg and skip ticket for the rest of the sprint.
